user_request:
I want to create a table tracking AI companies that are actively hiring.

Columns needed:
- Company Name
- Website
- Is hiring for AI roles? (yes/no)
- Team size (approximate)
- Recent funding (last round)

Find about 10 companies across different AI sectors like research, healthcare, and enterprise.
Make sure to include both established companies and startups.

columns.name: Table Configuration ID
columns.description: Unique identifier for this research table configuration

columns.name: Entity Type
columns.description: The type of entities this table will track (e.g., companies, papers, people, products)

columns.name: Research Objective
columns.description: Clear statement of what this table aims to discover or track about the entities
columns.validation_strategy: Verify objective is specific, measurable, and aligned with column definitions; check that all columns support the stated objective

columns.name: Primary Search Domain
columns.description: The main domain or industry focus for entity discovery (e.g., AI/ML, Healthcare, Finance)
columns.validation_strategy: Confirm domain is well-defined and that search subdomains align with this primary focus

columns.name: Number of Subdomains
columns.description: Count of parallel search subdomains for row discovery (2-5 recommended)
columns.validation_strategy: Verify count is between 2-5; confirm each subdomain is distinct and non-overlapping

columns.name: Target Row Count
columns.description: Desired number of entities to populate this table after deduplication and scoring
columns.validation_strategy: Check that target is realistic given search scope; verify discovery multiplier (1.5x) is applied to candidate search

columns.name: Data Findability Assessment
columns.description: Assessment of whether column data can be found through public web search without insider knowledge
columns.validation_strategy: Review each column's validation_strategy to ensure data sources are publicly accessible; flag any columns requiring private/insider data

columns.name: Search Query Quality Score
columns.description: Assessment of search queries for multi-row yield and relevance (1-10 scale)
columns.validation_strategy: Evaluate each search query: prioritize list/directory queries (10 points), aggregator sources (8 points), comparative queries (6 points), category queries (4 points); penalize single-entity queries (1 point)

columns.name: Validation Strategy Completeness
columns.description: Whether all research columns have specific, actionable validation strategies defined
columns.validation_strategy: Verify every column with is_identification=false has a validation_strategy field; check strategies are specific and actionable (not generic)

search_strategy.description: Since no specific user requirements were provided, this search strategy demonstrates the framework for discovering research table configurations. The strategy identifies configurations across different entity types, research objectives, and domain focuses. It uses parallel subdomains to discover configurations suitable for various research scenarios, from company tracking to academic research to product analysis.

subdomains.name: Company & Organization Tracking
subdomains.focus: Research table configurations designed for tracking companies, startups, enterprises, and organizations across various industries and stages
search_queries: how to create company research tracking spreadsheet
company research columns and metrics to track

subdomains.name: Academic & Research Entity Tracking
subdomains.focus: Research table configurations for tracking academic papers, researchers, research institutions, and scientific publications

subdomains.name: Product & Technology Tracking
subdomains.focus: Research table configurations for tracking software products, technology solutions, tools, and digital services

subdomains.name: Market & Competitive Intelligence
subdomains.focus: Research table configurations for competitive analysis, market research, and industry benchmarking

final_rows.match_rationale: Crunchbase is a primary source for company and startup data, highly relevant and reliable, but the exact table configuration is not specified in the source.
PitchBook provides detailed company and investment tracking, highly relevant and reliable, but specific table schema is not detailed in the source.
CB Insights offers comprehensive company and market intelligence, highly relevant and reliable, but exact table structure is not specified.
AngelList is a leading platform for startup tracking, highly relevant, but as a secondary source, reliability is slightly lower.
Capterra specializes in software and technology product listings, user reviews, and comparison tools, aligning well with the research framework.

id_values.Authors: Qingxiu Dong , Lei Li , Damai Dai , Ce Zheng , Zhiyong Wu , Baobao Chang , Xu Sun , Jingjing Xu , Lei Li , Zhifang Sui
id_values.Abstract: With the increasing ability of large language models (LLMs), in-context learning (ICL) has become a new paradigm for natural language processing (NLP), where LLMs make predictions based on contexts augmented with just a few training examples. This paper surveys the progress, challenges, and future work in ICL, and discusses the relationship between ICL and the pretrain–finetune paradigm.
id_values.Keywords: In-context learning, large language models, natural language processing, survey

final_rows.match_rationale: This paper is a comprehensive survey on in-context learning, directly addressing the topic of the query and providing a broad overview of the field, including challenges and future directions. The source is a preprint from arXiv, which is a reputable repository for academic papers, though not a peer-reviewed journal. The publication date is recent (July 2022).
G2 is a leading platform for software and technology product reviews and comparisons, directly matching the research objectives for product and technology tracking.
TrustRadius provides in-depth reviews and comparisons of business software and technology solutions, supporting comprehensive product analysis.
Gartner Peer Insights offers verified user reviews and ratings for enterprise software and technology, suitable for academic and company research.
GetApp provides software comparisons, user reviews, and research tools for technology solutions, fitting the product tracking framework.
